Grammar of Meaning
Thurman · source-board · pick the water

Pick the water

The piece eyedroppers its palette from a real source, then harmonizes with the house. Stills → the target still now; video → the live pixelize (build-SB). All public-domain / CC — license on each.

§13 — every item verified PD / CC0 / CC-BY on its own Commons page. ShareAlike + attribution flagged where they apply.
— strong picks first · then the rest —
STILL ★ strong pick

Belharra — Basque giant-wave, Bay of Biscay

steel-teal wall + churned white spray crest + near-black trough shadow — a deep cold ground with one luminous foam accent

source ↗
Creative Commons Attribution 2.0 Generic (CC BY 2.0) — attribute Gadjo Niglo

STILL ★ strong pick

Reynisfjara — surf on black basalt, Iceland

black volcanic rock + slate-grey cold swell + bright white breaking foam — the darkest ground of the set, spray as the glow

source ↗
Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-SA 2.0) — attribute sergejf; ShareAlike

STILL ★ strong pick

Dark ocean swell — Jon Sullivan (open-water)

deep petrol-blue swell + glassy mid-tone face + white capping foam — cleanest PD photographic still; no rights friction

source ↗
Public Domain — released worldwide by author Jon Sullivan (no attribution required)

STILL

Stormy Sea at Night — Aivazovsky, 1849 (painting)

near-black storm ground + moon-gold luminous break in the cloud + green-black wave faces — painterly but exactly the register; eyedropper yields dramatic dark + one warm glow

source ↗
Public Domain (PD-Art) — artist d. 1900; no rights

STILL

The Great Wave off Kanagawa — Hokusai (woodblock)

Prussian-blue deep water + pale sky + white claw-foam — flat graphic palette; the iconic dark-water motif if you want a stylized (not photographic) source

source ↗
Public Domain — published before 1931; no rights

STILL

Moonlight on the Bering Sea — Nowell, 1906 (B&W)

near-black sea + silver moon-track highlight (monochrome) — pure dark-ground + one luminous line; you'd tint the mono into the house palette

source ↗
Public Domain — photographer d. 1950, published before 1931; no rights

VIDEO

Storm on the Black Sea — 4K storm footage

grey-green storm swell + churning white chop + dark troughs — live motion; strongest VIDEO for the build-SB pixelize (modern 4K, genuinely turbulent)

source ↗
Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported (CC BY 3.0) — attribute Евгений (SuperZhekis). 1:04, 3840×2160

VIDEO

A Storm at Sea, 1900 — Edison, Atlantic crossing

silver-grey monochrome heaving sea (camera lashed to a ship's bridge) — archival grain; register-perfect mood but very low resolution — better as texture than sharp target

source ↗
Public Domain (US) — Edison Mfg. Co., 1900; copyright expired. 1:20, 320×240 (low-res archival)

VIDEO

Waves of the sea — 1080p wave clip

mid blue-grey rolling swell + white foam — clean 1080p motion, but lighter/less dramatic than the Black Sea clip; ShareAlike adds a license constraint

source ↗
Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-SA 4.0) — attribution + ShareAlike. 0:18, 1920×1080

HOW PRODUCED — design-SB source-board, 2026-07-03, per researcher: eyedropper method (pull a deep ground + a luminous accent from a real source) + upgrade-the-source (start from genuine cold/dramatic water, not a bright stock beach) + video-source idea (a live clip feeds the build-SB pixelize). Thumbnails fetched from Wikimedia Commons via Special:FilePath / thumb endpoints, embedded as base64 data URIs so the board is self-contained.

SCHOLARLY SOURCES — each item's source URL + license lives verbatim on its card. Register target = New England / Scotland / Big Sur / Iceland / open-Atlantic cold, dark, dramatic, luminous deep water (NOT bright tropical). PD photographic stills (Jon Sullivan open-water; Bering Sea moonlight) carry zero rights friction; PD-Art / PD-woodblock (Aivazovsky, Hokusai) are stylized-but-on-register; CC-BY / CC-BY-SA items note attribution + ShareAlike obligations.

WHAT NEEDS VERIFICATION — re-verify the final pick's license on its own Commons page immediately before use (Commons license tags can change on re-upload). Video source = build-SB live pixelize (Black Sea 4K is the strongest motion candidate; Waves-of-the-sea is CC-BY-SA / ShareAlike; 1900 Edison clip is PD but only 320×240 — texture, not a sharp target). Still = eyedropper target now. No new corpus rows written; Stage 6 = 0 (this is a design artifact, not coded data).
